title: Roasted Polish Potato Roast
description: A hearty, molten roast featuring Dutch stroop and confit tomato, served in a tall talavera plate.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a tall talavera plate with parchment paper.
Peel the potatoes and cut them into 1‑inch cubes, rinsing in cold water to remove excess starch.
Pat the potato cubes dry with a clean towel, then toss them in a bowl with olive oil, sea salt, pepper, minced garlic, thyme, smoked paprika, and a splash of dutch stroop.
Spread the seasoned potatoes in an even layer on the prepared plate, ensuring they are not crowded.
Roast in the oven for 25 minutes, turning once, until the edges are golden and the cores are tender.
Remove the plate, drizzle confit tomato over the potatoes, and sprinkle chopped parsley for freshness.
Return to the oven for an additional 5 minutes to meld the flavors, then let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Slice the roasted potato roast into generous portions, placing each piece on a serving platter.
Drizzle a small amount of residual dutch stroop over each serving to enhance sweetness.
Serve hot, accompanied by a side of sautéed kale or a simple green salad.
